decisions: Turn 1: Labeling schema fixed at two fields. platform_category (Chzzk videoCategoryValue) is authoritative and is what axis_coverage_ok / promotion gating evaluates. content_judgement is an optional annotation for human readability and is not used for promotion math.

Turn 1: Chzzk chats API is cookieless. W5 chat acquired directly via pipeline.chat_collector.fetch_all_chats. W4 offset required paginate-from-t=0 plus window-filter because the API does not honor arbitrary playerMessageTime seek values.

Turn 1: Both cells now have chat (W4f 81.52 msgs/min high; W5f 51.48 msgs/min medium). Both cells insufficient_data=false on re-measure. W4f chunk 1 reproduced the within-chunk retry-collapse pattern (cache populated by initial warm before 5s retry sleep), but 3 other chunks passed on first pair so the cell still landed valid.

Turn 1: Global decision stays per_cell_multiplicative. covered_cell_count=2 within this session. Cross-session aggregation across {W1, W2, W3, W4f, W5f} = 5 valid cells if executed, but is deferred to either the smoke contract or a tiny follow-up Turn 2.

Turn 2: Peer verification accepted the execution thesis and corrected two wording-level overclaims. Next work is not another acquisition session: attach the deterministic cross-session aggregation step to the smoke contract as Step 0, then run the end-to-end smoke.
meta_improvements: Chzzk chats API seek semantics: only nextPlayerMessageTime from a prior page reliably yields the desired relative offset slice. An arbitrary playerMessageTime request can return a page, but not the requested relative window. Record this in any future offset-pull helper.

Chat-acquisition feasibility should be part of the execute session's preflight, not discovered as a no-chat gap afterward. The 'W5 is no-chat' framing from the prior session was a data-absence artifact, not a property of the VOD.

experiments/a4_measure.py within-chunk retry uses time.sleep(5). With the Anthropic prompt cache 5-minute TTL, the initial warm call populates the cache before retry_cold fires, collapsing retry_user_attributable to 2. A MIN_RETRY_IDLE_SEC ~330-360s guard or a prefix-perturbation trick would fix this.

cross-session aggregation across sessions needs an explicit runner. evaluate_global in a4_measure.py currently takes per-session cell summaries. A small experiments/ script that reads two or more raw JSONs and feeds their cells into one evaluate_global call would close the 'covered_cell_count>=5 cross-session' gate.

Documentary claims about HEAD byte-identity must distinguish tracked files from untracked sister-only carry-forward artifacts. The latter can be preserved and carried forward, but HEAD identity is undefined.
